concerns.text: Task T021 (Inter-family generalization test) is marked incomplete (unchecked). The spec defines 'Demonstrate generalization to unseen chemical families' and the Constitution (FR-030, Principle VI) mandates that the accuracy claim is valid ONLY when evaluated against held-out DFT values from explicitly excluded families. Without T021, the primary success criterion and constitutional validity of the accuracy claim are unverified.

Tasks T015a, T022a, and T028a (explicit 'Surrogate Model' disclaimers in code/docs) are marked incomplete. The Spec Constraints and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) require explicit distinction between 'first-principles' and 'surrogate' methods. Leaving these unchecked creates a semantic gap where the implementation may silently revert to 'First-Principles' terminology, violating the core project definition.

Task T027 (Report Generation) is incomplete. The Spec Success Criteria require identifying 'key structural descriptors'. Constitution Principle VII mandates that every claim regarding feature importance MUST be substantiated by SHAP/permutation values. T027 is the only task synthesizing these into a report; its absence means the project cannot satisfy the attribution constraint.

Tasks T001 (Project Structure) and T003 (Linting/Formatting) are marked complete (checked) but the 'Analyze Report' indicates they were rejected for missing artifacts. Marking tasks as complete when the required artifacts (directory structure, config files) do not exist violates the constraint that task deliverables must match the spec's reproducibility requirements (Constitution Principle I).
